skill.name: article-format-adjustment
skill.description: Adjust academic paper formatting and convert between DOCX/LaTeX/Markdown when you need to meet a journal or school template requirement.
skill.summary: Automates academic paper formatting and document conversion to meet journal, conference, or school template requirements.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Evaluated 142 static analysis findings across 28 files (3334 lines). All 20 'high' severity findings ('Weak cryptographic algorithm') are FALSE POSITIVES - the scanner matched on journal formatting configuration files (font names, margin values), argparse help text strings, and YAML frontmatter descriptions that contain no cryptographic code. Both 'critical' obfuscation heuristics ('DANGEROUS COMBINATION') are FALSE POSITIVES - the combination of subprocess (pandoc/latex), network (journal template downloads from known academic domains), and dict config access is legitimate academic paper formatting behavior. The 84 'medium' external_commands findings in SKILL.md are documented pandoc/latex shell commands for document conversion, not arbitrary command injection. The 'env_access' detection (7 locations) at template_downloader.py:330-336 is also a FALSE POSITIVE - these are Python dict config.get() calls, not os.environ or os.getenv reads. No evidence of credential exfiltration, malicious network destinations, or command injection. Confirmed risk factors are limited to legitimate document conversion operations: subprocess.run for pandoc, temp file management during conversions, and network access for journal template downloads.

low_findings.title: Shell command execution in SKILL.md instructions
low_findings.description: SKILL.md contains backtick-wrapped shell commands for pandoc, latexmk, and document conversion operations. Arguments are fixed and documented within the skill instructions. These commands are part of the skill's intended functionality for academic paper format conversion and do not accept arbitrary user input. Legitimate usage pattern.

low_findings.title: subprocess.run for external tool invocation
low_findings.description: subprocess.run is called at format_converter.py:334 to check pandoc version (hardcoded argument '--version'). No user input injection vector - arguments are fixed strings. Pandoc is a required external tool for document format conversion between DOCX, LaTeX, and Markdown.

low_findings.title: Network access to journal template URLs
low_findings.description: Template downloader connects to official journal websites (nature.com, sciencemag.org, springer.com, thelancet.com, bmj.com, ieee.org) to download formatting templates. All URLs point to legitimate publisher resources, not data exfiltration endpoints.

low_findings.title: Temporary file creation during format conversion
low_findings.description: tempfile module is imported and used in format_converter.py and init_run.py for creating temporary files during document conversion operations. Standard practice for file format transformation workflows.

content.user_title: Format academic papers for any journal requirement
content.value_statement: Researchers spend hours manually adjusting paper formatting to meet journal guidelines. This skill automates academic paper formatting and document conversion between DOCX, LaTeX, and Markdown using journal-specific templates.
actual_capabilities: Convert academic papers between DOCX, LaTeX, and Markdown formats
Apply formatting from built-in journal templates including Nature, Cell, PLOS, IEEE, and APA
Adjust document structure including title page, headings, fonts, margins, and line spacing
Download and apply the latest journal formatting templates from official sources
Validate formatted output against configured formatting rules and report issues
Create custom formatting templates using JSON or YAML configuration files
limitations: Does not handle complex custom formatting rules beyond the supported journal templates
Format conversion may lose some document elements such as embedded images or complex nested tables
Requires internet access to download journal templates from official sources
Does not review or validate scientific content, data accuracy, or citation correctness

use_cases.title: Journal submission preparation
use_cases.description: Format a research manuscript to meet a specific journal's submission guidelines by applying the correct typography, citation style, and section layout.

use_cases.title: Document format migration
use_cases.description: Convert existing academic papers between formats when changing journals, publication venues, or when collaborators require a different file format.

use_cases.title: Standardized lab paper formatting
use_cases.description: Apply consistent formatting templates across multiple papers from the same research group for conference proceedings or thesis requirements.

prompt_templates.title: Convert my paper to LaTeX
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a research paper in DOCX format that I need to convert to LaTeX. Please convert the file and preserve all section headings, citations, and references.
prompt_templates.scenario: A beginner user needs to convert a Word document to LaTeX for journal submission.

prompt_templates.title: Apply a journal template
prompt_templates.prompt: Please format my paper using the Nature journal template. Apply the correct font sizes, margins, spacing, and citation style. The paper covers molecular biology research.
prompt_templates.scenario: An intermediate user needs to apply a specific journal's formatting rules to their manuscript.

prompt_templates.title: Create a custom template
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a custom formatting template with Times New Roman 12pt body text, double line spacing, 1-inch margins, and APA citation style. Save it for future use on my lab papers.
prompt_templates.scenario: An advanced user needs to define and save custom formatting rules for repeated use.

prompt_templates.title: Batch format multiple papers
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the IEEE conference template to all three papers in the conference_submissions folder. Convert each to PDF after formatting and report any validation issues found.
prompt_templates.scenario: An expert user needs to process multiple papers with the same template and validate all outputs.

output_examples.input: My paper.docx file needs to be converted to LaTeX format for journal submission.
output_examples.output: The paper was converted successfully. The DOCX file was processed through the conversion pipeline: all section headings became LaTeX section commands, inline citations were converted to \cite{key} format, the bibliography was formatted as a .bib file, and the main document structure was wrapped in \documentclass{article}. The output .tex file is ready for compilation with pdflatex.

output_examples.input: Please format my manuscript using the Cell Press journal template.
output_examples.output: I applied the Cell Press formatting template to your manuscript. Title font changed to Arial 14pt bold, body text to Times New Roman 10pt, line spacing set to double, margins adjusted to 2.54cm all around, and citations converted to the Cell numbered style. Figure captions were moved to below each figure and table captions to above each table. The formatted document is ready for submission.

best_practices: Always validate your formatted output against the target journal's official author guidelines before submission
Keep original versions of your papers before applying format conversions in case you need to revert changes
Test the skill on a single paper first before batch processing multiple manuscripts
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill to fabricate research data, results, or citations - it only adjusts formatting
Do not use this skill without the required input files, templates, or credentials needed for download
Do not rely solely on automatic validation - manually verify complex formatting such as table layouts and math equations

faq.question: Which document formats are supported?
faq.answer: The skill supports DOCX (Word), LaTeX (TEX), and Markdown (MD) formats. It can convert between any of these three formats.

faq.question: How do I select a journal template?
faq.answer: You can specify a journal name using the --journal parameter. Built-in templates include Nature, Cell, PLOS, IEEE, APA, and many others.

faq.question: Do I need to install any external tools?
faq.answer: Pandoc is required for conversions involving LaTeX and DOCX formats. Other conversions work with the built-in Python scripts.

faq.question: Can I create my own formatting rules?
faq.answer: Yes, you can create custom formatting templates using JSON or YAML configuration files with settings for fonts, margins, spacing, and citation styles.

faq.question: Will my figures and tables be preserved during conversion?
faq.answer: Basic figures and tables are preserved, but complex nested tables or embedded objects may lose some formatting during conversion.

faq.question: What happens if the formatting validation fails?
faq.answer: The tool reports which formatting rules were not met, including details about font sizes, margins, spacing, and citation styles that need correction.
